summary: Uncle Bob announces a new initiative to fly his personal airplane to clients within 500 nautical miles of Chicago, offering his talks and training at half price. The catch is flexibility on scheduling due to weather dependencies and his preference to return home each night. He frames this as both an opportunity to enjoy his new pilot's license and a way to serve the underserved 'fly-over country' that rarely gets visits from industry speakers and authors.
key_points: Offering half-price talks and training to anyone within 500 nautical miles of Chicago
Scheduling flexibility required due to weather-dependent flying conditions
Prefers day trips to be home with family each evening
Sees the Midwest as underserved by tech industry speakers and wants to change that
Suggests local airports as potential venues for user group workshops
money_quotes: I just got my pilot's license. That means I can fly! So now I want to fly – to you!
Sorry, I'm not flying through thunder storms to get to you. No. Not going to happen. Deal with it!
I love my wife! I love my family! What can I say? I want to be home.
Flying is a hoot! I enjoy the hell out of it.
I live smack in the middle of fly-over country. This, it seems to me, is an area of the country that is badly underserved by speakers like me.
There are lots of programmers inside that circle who never see authors, or speakers, or industry experts. I'd like to do my part to change that.
key_insight: Uncle Bob sees an opportunity to combine his love of flying with serving programmers in the underserved Midwest who rarely get access to industry thought leaders.
